entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The primary cereal crop analyzed in the value chain study in Tanzania

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: The country where the study on the sorghum commodity value chain was conducted

entities.name: SORGHUM VALUE CHAIN
entities.description: The series of activities and stakeholders involved in the production and distribution of sorghum

entities.name: IMPROVED SORGHUM SEEDS
entities.description: Enhanced seed varieties designed to increase productivity and profitability for farmers

entities.name: NACO MTAMA 1
entities.description: A specific improved variety of sorghum with the highest adoption rate among farmers

entities.name: ADOPTION RATE
entities.description: The proportion of farmers who have transitioned to using improved sorghum varieties

entities.name: PROFITABILITY
entities.description: The positive gross benefits obtained by farmers and seed producers using improved seeds

entities.name: SOCIO-ECONOMIC FACTORS
entities.description: Variables such as education level and group membership that influence the adoption of improved seeds

entities.name: MARKET ACCESSIBILITY
entities.description: The ease with which farmers can reach markets to sell their sorghum

entities.name: SEED ACCESSIBILITY
entities.description: The ease with which farmers can obtain improved sorghum seeds

entities.name: FINANCIAL CONSTRAINTS
entities.description: Economic limitations that act as inefficiencies within the sorghum value chain

entities.name: POOR LINKAGES
entities.description: Lack of effective coordination and communication among stakeholders in the value chain

entities.name: GRAIN QUALITY
entities.description: The physical and chemical characteristics of the sorghum grain affecting its marketability

entities.name: TAX LEVIES
entities.description: Government-imposed taxes that create challenges for traders in the value chain
